msgs.text:
I found a concrete obstruction to the Turn 1 / Turn 2 candidate. It is not just unproved; in its present form it fails a finite-index test.

The prior candidate was sigma,tau,x,y ytau = (y*tau)^40491355905 x^2*y^3*ytau^sigma where 40491355905 is the verifier-modulus representative of the pro-2 idempotent π2. This is compatible with the prompt syntax and with the implicit tame relation τσ=τ2, as the prior turns noted.The defect already appears after killing τ and passing to the unramified quadratic tame quotient, so the (yτ)π2 norm term is irrelevant here: it becomes just y. Thus the candidate gives the base pro-2 relationr=x2y3yσ.Let U be the open subgroup corresponding to the unramified quadratic extension, i.e. the kernel of the tame quotient map with σ↦1∈C2. Schreier generators may be taken asx0=x,y0=y,x1=xσ,y1=yσ,S=σ2.The two conjugate relators have, up to harmless convention on conjugation by S,r0=x02y03y1,r1=x12y13y0S.This is exactly the unramified-degree-2 case of the prior candidate’s tame-open-subgroup construction.Now take the index-2 subgroup V⊂U defined by the mod-2 characterφ(x0)=φ(x1)=1,φ(y0)=φ(y1)=φ(S)=0.

Using transversal {1,x0}, convenient Schreier generators for V area=x02,u=x1x0−1,v=x0x1,b0=y0, c0=y1, s0=S,b1=x0y0x0−1, c1=x0y1x0−1, s1=x0Sx0−1.

After abelianizing V, the four Schreier relators givea+3b0+c0=0,a+3b1+c1=0,u+v+b0+3c0=0,u+v+b1+3c1=0.

Subtracting the first two and the last two relations, withB=b0−b1,C=c0−c1,gives 3B+C=0,B+3C=0.

The relation matrix is(3113),whose Smith form over Z2 is diag(1,8). ThereforeVab≃Z25⊕Z/8Z.This is impossible for the actual absolute Galois group. If U were GL (2) for the unramified quadratic extension L/Q2, then every index-2 subgroup V would be GE (2) for some quadratic extension E/L. By local class field theory, the abelianization of GE (2) is the pro-2 completion of E×, with torsion μ2∞ (E); Labute records this as (Z/qZ)×Zpd+1, where q is the highest p-power root-of-unity order in the field.Cambridge University Press & Assessment

But no quadratic E/L can contain μ8. Indeed, Q2(ζ8)/Q2 has degree 4 and is totally ramified: Φ8 (T+1)=(T+1)4+1=T4+4T3+6T2+4T+2 is Eisenstein at 2. Since L/Q2 is unramified quadratic, L∩Q2(ζ8)=Q2, so L(ζ8)/L has degree 4. A quadratic extension E/L therefore cannot contain ζ8. Its pro-2 abelianized Galois group can have torsion at most Z/4Z, not Z/8Z.So the candidate from Turns 1–2 realizes the wrong cyclotomic orientation over the unramified quadratic subgroup. This also explains why the earlier tests were misleading: the base maximal pro-2 quotient, H1 -rank, and degree-2 cup product can all look right while the higher 2-adic orientation invariant is wrong. Labute’s classification explicitly requires the image of the character χ in the q=2 cases; for even degree, the full-image case has relation type x12(x1,x2)x32f(x3,x4)⋯, not merely the same quadratic initial form.Cambridge University Press & Assessment

ESTABLISHED: The Turn 1 / Turn 2 candidate is refuted. In its unramified quadratic open subgroup it has an index-2 subgroup with abelianization Z25⊕Z/8, impossible for any quadratic extension of the unramified quadratic 2-adic field.

GAP: A replacement relation is still needed. The failed ansatz used a Labute-normal-form-looking generator in the role of the tame Frobenius; the tame Frobenius must instead have the correct cyclotomic orientation over unramified subextensions.

NEXT: Rebuild the base pro-2 relation with σ constrained to be a tame Frobenius lift, then re-run the unramified quadratic Schreier test before considering odd tame ramification.[BREAKTHROUGH]
